themes: Claude Code pricing blunder (Pro plan removal)

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Best single take on the Claude Code Pro plan removal: Simon flags the contradiction that $20/mo still gets you Cowork, which is essentially Claude Code rebranded — making the move strategically incoherent.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon's read that this was an ill-considered A/B test — and the direct warning that OpenAI Codex will catch Claude Code fast if Anthropic follows through — is the sharpest competitive framing in the feed.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Nails the funnel logic problem: nobody cold-converts to $100/mo without a $20 trial tier — a product strategy failure that matters for anyone thinking about PLG and AI tool adoption.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Breaks down the SpaceX-Cursor deal economics: $60B buyout option or $10B walk-away, with context on Cursor's valuation trajectory — the most substantive coverage of this partnership in the feed.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Reframes OpenAI's Codex compute reset announcement as a direct poaching play aimed at rate-limited Claude Code power users — useful competitive intelligence for AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Anthropic launched a prompt cache dashboard in the Developer Console — actionable for anyone building on the Claude API who wants to cut costs and TTFT.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: The math on Claude Code's Max plan vs. API pricing (10B tokens, $200/mo vs. $15K at API rates) is the clearest articulation of why Anthropic's subscription tiers are structurally underpriced.

worthClicking.author: @jeff_weinstein
worthClicking.why: Active recruiting post for developers building economic agents with checkout integration — signals where the agentic commerce layer is actually being built right now.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: One-line market map that cuts through the noise: Anthropic comes for Figma, OpenAI comes for Canva — worth reading the 68-reply thread for the product strategy debate.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Crisp thesis on which software categories AI destroys first: single-player, comprehension-bound tools — directly relevant for indie dev and AI product strategy.

voices.why: Most reliable real-time analyst on Anthropic/OpenAI product moves; caught the Claude Code pricing change early and provided the clearest competitive framing.
Consistently translates AI platform economics and PM role evolution into concrete numbers — heavy volume but the AI-relevant posts are genuinely useful.
Building at the agentic commerce layer (economic agents, machine payments) and asking the right infrastructure questions — low volume, high signal for distribution strategy.
Sharp product strategy takes, especially on competitive displacement and distribution — punchy, original, not derivative.

examples: aakashgupta newsletter self-promotion posts (5+ identical CTA tweets)
RoundtableSpace all-caps sensationalism (AI grief clones, crypto lawsuits, trading algos)
minchoi GPT-Image-2 listicle thread (10 numbered posts with no substantive content)

emerging.theme: Claude Code pricing blunder (Pro plan removal)
